---
title: "fMRI Windowing"
subtitle: "`RunGrid`, `ExperimentGrid`, `query_feature_window_tr()` - stimulus features on run clocks."
sidebar: concepts
---

## What it is

fMRI windowing helpers describe run timing separately from feature timing. A `RunGrid` stores the TR, run start, run length, and feature-time offset. Query helpers slice feature objects by run and optionally resample to the run TR grid.

## Invariants

- `tr_s` must be positive.
- `n_trs_by_run` must contain positive integers.
- `run_starts_s`, when supplied, must match the number of runs.
- `feature_t0_by_run`, when supplied, must match the number of runs.
- Query windows require `t_end_s > t_start_s`.
- Output time can be `absolute`, `run_relative`, or `feature`.

## What it refuses

- Unknown run indices.
- Empty or non-positive run definitions.
- Invalid output-time modes.
- Windows whose end is not later than their start.

## What it doesn't do

Windowing does not estimate an HRF model. It slices and samples feature objects so modeling code can consume run-aware feature matrices.

## See also

[Query a run-aware fMRI window](../cookbook/fmri-window-query.qmd) · [fMRI querying reference](../reference/query_feature_window_tr.qmd)
